subject: Chem Y1
text: But-1-ene, but-2-ene and methylpropene are isomeric alkenes. Which isomer has E/Z isomerism?
explain: E/Z isomerism requires a C=C with TWO different groups on EACH carbon. But-2-ene: each C of C=C has H and CH3. But-1-ene: C1 has 2H (same), no E/Z. Methylpropene: C2 has 2CH3 groups (same).
improve: For E/Z: each C in C=C must have 2 DIFFERENT groups
mscheme: Correct answer is B. But-2-ene: each C of C=C has H and CH3 (2 different groups) = E/Z possible

subject: Chem Y1
text: In the reaction: MnO4- + 5Fe2+ + 8H+ -> Mn2+ + 5Fe3+ + 4H2O, what is the oxidation number change of Mn?
explain: The only correct answer is A (heat in a sealed tube, ethanol). A: cyclohexane is not a good solvent for Grignard reagents as it is not polar; C: ethyl ethanoate would react with Grignard reagents; D: hexane is not a good solvent for Grignard reagents as it is not polar
improve: Mn in MnO4- is +7. Mn2+ is +2. Mn is reduced (gains 5e)
mscheme: the following points: • (order with respect to hydrogen peroxide =) 1 / first • (order with respect to iodide ions =) 1 / first (1) (1) (2) Question Number Answer

subject: Chem Y1
text: Which species has bond angles of approximately 109.5 AND 120 degrees?
explain: The only correct answer is D (Ca2+). A: S2– has fewer protons than Ca2+ and has the largest ionic radius of those listed; B: Cl− has fewer protons than Ca2+ and has the second largest ionic radius; C: K+ has fewer protons than Ca2+ and has the second smallest ionic radius; A: these are the successive ionisation energies of a Group 3 element; B: these are the successive ionisation energies of a transition element; C: these are the successive ionisation energies of a Group 2 element
improve: Look for molecules with DIFFERENT regions of electron density around different atoms
mscheme: Correct answer is C. CH3COOH (ethanoic acid): CH3 tetrahedral (109.5 deg), COOH trig planar (120 deg)

subject: Chem Y1
text: For the equilibrium 2SO2(g) + O2(g) = 2SO3(g), what is the effect of increasing pressure on yield of SO3?
options: Yield increases (equilibrium shifts right - fewer moles of gas)
explain: The only correct answer is C (hydrogen bonding, permanent dipole-dipole forces and London forces). B: the equation shows 2 mol NH3 so the term [NH3] must be squared not doubled , and ammonium carbamate is a solid so it does not appear in the equilibrium expression D is not correct because ammonium carbamate is a solid so it should not be included in the equilibrium expression; A: although hydrogen bonding is the strongest intermolecular force, permanent dipole-dipole interactions and London forces are also present; B: London forces are always present between molecules in the liquid state; D: London forces are the weakest of the intermolecular forces, and other intermolecular forces are also present between butan-1-ol molecules; A: hydrogen chloride is not white smoke; C: carbonates only react with acids and CO2 cannot be observed; D: carbonates only react with acids
improve: Le Chatelier: increase pressure shifts to side with fewer gas moles
mscheme: Mark scheme: Correct answer is C

subject: Chem Y1
text: The enthalpy change of combustion of methane is -890 kJ/mol. What does the negative sign indicate?
options: The reaction absorbs heat from surroundings
The reaction releases heat to surroundings
explain: The only correct answer is A (0.0233). B: the value assumes the formula of calcium hydroxide is CaOH; C: the value assumes that 17.3 g is the mass of calcium not calcium hydroxide; D: this assumes the relative formula mass of calcium hydroxide is 29 (the sum of the atomic numbers)
improve: Negative delta-H = exothermic (heat out). Positive = endothermic (heat in)
mscheme: the following points: • decrease the concentration (of the nitric acid) / use ethanoic acid / a weak acid • decrease in reaction rate would result in slower formation of carbon dioxide (so less gas lost) (1) (1) Allow use a different acid that is less reactive M2 dependent on M1 Allow just results in a decrease in reaction rate Allow it will take longer to react (2) Question Number Answer

subject: Stats Y1
text: 1/7 of packets contain a prize. 40 packets per box. Using a suitable approximation for X~B(40,1/7), find P(X<=3).
explain: (a) Comment in context about either independence or random packing e.g. “prizes must be placed in packets at random/independently of each other” or about constant probability e.g. “the probability of a packet containing a prize is constant/ the same/fixed” B1 3.5b (1) (b)(i) [P(T = 6) = ] 0.17273… awrt 0.173 B1 1.1b (ii) [P(T < 3) = P(T 2 „ ) = ] 0.061587… awrt 0.0616 B1 1.1b (2) (c) [K= no. of boxes with fewer than 3 packets containing a prize] K ~ B(5, “0.0616”) M1 1.1b P(K = 2) = 0.031344… in the range [0.0313~0.0314] A1 1.1b (2) (d) 1 1 0 1 7 7 : H : H p p =  B1 2.5 [X = no of packets containing a prize] X~B(110, 1 7 ) M1 3.3 [P(X 9 „ )] = 0.038292… A1 3.4 [Significant result or reject 0 H ] E.g. there is evidence to support Kamil’s claim A1 2.2b (4) ( 9 marks)
improve: B(n,p): if n is large and p small, approximate with Po(np)
mscheme: Mark scheme: For binomial B(n,p): mean=np, variance=np(1-p). Use these to find n and p.

subject: Mech Y1
text: Two particles connected by light inextensible string over smooth pulley. Masses 3kg and 5kg. Acceleration?
improve: Atwood machine: a = (m2-m1)g/(m1+m2)

subject: Chem Y1
text: Which is a reduction step?
explain: The only correct answer is D (permanent dipoles). A: there are no covalent bonds between molecules. B: this molecule does not contain hydrogen so there are no hydrogen bonds between molecules. C: there are no ionic bonds between molecules
improve: Reduction: oxidation state DECREASES (gains e-). Oxidation: oxidation state INCREASES (loses e-)
mscheme:
Correct answer is D. A: there are no covalent bonds between molecules. B: this molecule does not contain hydrogen so there are no hydrogen bonds between molecules. C: there are no ionic bonds between molecules  
 
(1).

subject: Chem Y1
text: Ethanol can be produced by fermentation. What are the essential conditions?
explain: Fermentation: yeast, 30-40 deg C (enzyme optimum), anaerobic (no oxygen). C6H12O6 -> 2C2H5OH + 2CO2
improve: Fermentation conditions: yeast, warm (30-40), anaerobic
mscheme: Mark scheme:

subject: Chem Y1
text: Using Hess cycle, delta-H reaction = sum of delta-H products - sum of delta-H reactants. For CH4 + 2O2 -> CO2 + 2H2O, given delta-Hf: CH4=-74.8, CO2=-393.5, H2O=-285.8. Calculate delta-H reaction.
explain: The only correct answer is A (heat in a sealed tube, ethanol). A: cyclohexane is not a good solvent for Grignard reagents as it is not polar; C: ethyl ethanoate would react with Grignard reagents; D: hexane is not a good solvent for Grignard reagents as it is not polar
improve: delta-H rxn = sum(delta-Hf products) - sum(delta-Hf reactants). Elements have delta-Hf = 0
